section: Effects of Mn Contents on the Microstructure and Mechanical Properties of the Fe–10Al–xMn–1.0C Alloy

context_update.summary: This chunk covers the abstract, introduction, and experimental procedures of paper A46. It defines the alloy system Fe–10Al–xMn–1.0C with Mn from 5 to 40 mass%, produced by sheet casting (2.5×15×125 mm) via air induction melting under N₂ and investment casting (pouring 1500–1520°C, mold 1160–1180°C). The as-cast duplex γ+α microstructure and TEM-identified phase constituents (γ + κ + κ′ in γ, D03 + κ″ in α) are stated, with α fraction decreasing with Mn. The chunk reports overall mechanical property ranges (UTS 646–887 MPa, YS 575–824 MPa, elongation 16.3–29.2%, HRC 31–44, damping ratio 0.0603–0.0417, Young’s modulus 139–165 GPa), and a linear UTS–HRC relation (UTS = 20.93 HRC). The chemical compositions of eight alloys (A to H) are tabulated (Table 1). Detailed characterization methods (OM, SEM, TEM, tensile, hardness, modal analysis) and specimen preparation parameters are described. No micrographs or property-vs-Mn plots appear yet; subsequent chunks should present the OM, TEM, tensile curves, and damping/modulus data that support these claims and reveal the trends with Mn content.
microstructure: α volume fraction decreases with Mn increase

Material systems: Fe–10Al–xMn–1.0C (mass%)

Key variables: Mn content (5,10,15,20,25,30,35,40 mass%); Al ≈ 10 mass%; C ≈ 1.0 mass%

context_update.summary: This chunk presents the microstructural analysis (section 3.1) of the as-cast Fe–10Al–xMn–1.0C sheet alloys. Optical micrographs (Fig.1) show dendritic solidification and decreasing α phase fraction with Mn, quantified in Table 2 (e.g., 55.4% α at 5Mn to 0% at 40Mn), and a quadratic α% vs Mn% relationship is given. TEM of alloy C (15Mn) identifies the austenite region as containing κ′ coarse and κ fine (Fe,Mn)3AlCx carbides with ordered L′12 structure (a=0.383–0.386 nm) sharing the same orientation; satellites suggest spinodal decomposition. The coarse κ″ particles in α have L12 structure (a=0.387–0.389 nm). The α matrix is D03 (a=0.586–0.588 nm) with orientation relationship [1-11]κ‖[2-20]D03, (011)κ‖(001)D03. APB analysis indicates high-temperature disordered α and α→B2→D03 ordering. κ″ precipitates are more abundant at γ/α boundaries. No β-Mn phase is found. The overall microstructure is (γ+κ+κ′) + (D03+κ″) except for the 40Mn alloy, which is fully γ. The experimental modal method for Young's modulus and damping is described but no property data are yet reported. Subsequent chunks should provide tensile, hardness, damping, and modulus results vs. Mn content.
microstructure: duplex γ+α decreasing α with Mn
γ contains κ′ (coarse L′12) + κ (fine L′12)

context_update.summary: This chunk completes the microstructural description by stating the α→B2→D03 continuous ordering path and confirming all α regions are D03 with coarse κ″. It reiterates the overall duplex structure (γ+κ+κ′)+(D03+κ″) with only 40Mn being fully γ, notes higher κ″ density at γ/α boundaries, and absence of β-Mn. Section 3.2 reports full tensile and hardness data (Table 3, Fig.8): UTS peaks at 887 MPa for 15Mn, Y.S. 824 MPa, then decreases above 20Mn; elongation 16.3–29.2%; HRC 31–44 with UTS=20.93HRC. SEM fractographs (Fig.9) suggest (D03+κ″) regions influence fracture. Section 3.3 provides experimental modal results: natural frequencies 226–242 Hz, damping ratios 0.06–0.04 decreasing with Mn (Table 4, Fig.11), and Young’s modulus 139–165 GPa with a maximum of 159–165 GPa at 25–30Mn. These damping and modulus trends are claimed as novel. Subsequent chunks, if any, may contain conclusions synthesizing structure–property relations.
microstructure: higher κ″ density at γ/α boundaries
properties: maximum UTS 887 MPa, Y.S. 824 MPa at 15Mn
UTS and Y.S. decrease for Mn >20 mass%
damping ratio 0.0603–0.0417, decreases with Mn increase
Young’s modulus 139–165 GPa, peak 159–165 GPa at 25–30Mn
mechanisms: α→B2→D03 continuous ordering transition on quenching
(D03+κ″) two-phase region contributes to fracture mechanism

context_update.summary: The current chunk concludes the experimental results for Fe–10Al–xMn–1.0C sheet castings. Section 3.3 reports Young's modulus data (Table 4) ranging 139–165 GPa, with maximum 159–165 GPa at 25–30 mass% Mn; these results are noted as novel. Conclusions summarize the duplex microstructure of (γ+κ+κ′) and (D03+κ″) regions, with the D03+κ″ fraction decreasing with Mn and becoming fully γ at 40 mass% Mn. κ/κ′ carbides are ordered L'12 formed by spinodal decomposition; κ″ is L12 with lower carbon and random disorder. The α matrix forms D03 via α→B2→D03 ordering on quenching. Orientation relationship D03/κ″ defined. Mechanical properties range: HRC 31–44, UTS 646–887 MPa, YS 575–824 MPa, elongation 16.3–29.2%, damping 0.0417–0.0603, Young's modulus 139–165 GPa; maximum strength at ~15 mass% Mn. This chunk completes the property and microstructure summary; subsequent chunks are not expected.
properties: Young's modulus 139-165 GPa, max 159-165 GPa at 25-30Mn
max UTS and YS at 15 mass% Mn
